creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser presents a technique for sustaining software shipping with minimal human attention by dispatching agents to isolated git worktrees via voice briefs and managing them remotely. The system layers signal agents monitoring Slack and Linear, verification gates including lint and browser interaction, weekly JSONL history analysis for skill generation, and biometric feedback integration via MCP.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford from Snorkel demonstrates that a 4B model fine-tuned with reinforcement learning to use tools like `get_table_name` outperformed Qwen 3 235B on database query tasks, achieving correct answers through tool discipline and self-correction. The approach achieved 26.6% on FinQA with single-table transfer learning and cost under 500 dollars to train.

videos.title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemma 4's 31B model ranks fourth on the LM Arena leaderboard while running on a single GPU, achieving comparable quality to much larger competitors. The model is available as E2B and E4B variants for on-device deployment on Pixel phones and enables efficient deployment in medical and multi-agent scenarios.

videos.title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
videos.summary: PostHog built an automated pipeline that detects product signals like rage clicks and errors, groups them using LLM-generated embeddings, and generates pull requests to fix issues. The system uses background agents to analyze related errors, session replays, and codebase context, reducing manual triage and development cycles.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Building with Anthropic's Fable 5 LIVE - Is It Really Worth the Hype?
videos.summary: Cole Medin conducts a live test of Anthropic's Fable 5, evaluating whether the model's increased capability and 2x price premium over Claude Opus 4.8 justify the hype through real-world task performance.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open source tool that enables building and deploying AI agents to production with integrated identity management, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails. The CLI works with any AI coding assistant and simplifies the traditionally complex process of shipping production-grade AI agents.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live Q&A stream on Saturday covering AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream will be conducted while playing chess, with a focus on community engagement through the Dynamous community platform.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin demonstrates a workflow that routes different development phases to Claude Opus and Gemini 3.5 Flash based on each model's strengths: Opus for planning and integration logic, Gemini for frontend design and UI generation. The approach leverages Gemini's superior visual output and lower cost while using Opus for complex reasoning tasks.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: The video explains how to build proactive agents that can autonomously optimize systems through self-improving loops. It covers foundational concepts, implementation patterns, and practical use cases like autonomous ads optimization and SEO.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding features and practices, including the /goal feature and /Mission exploration for long-horizon tasks. The video explores tools and techniques that enable autonomous coder capabilities similar to or beyond Ralph-loop 2.0.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses OpenAI Symphony as a new coding paradigm that includes harness setup, auto-testing capabilities, and workflow management features. The video covers how Symphony works and provides setup instructions.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: A video showcasing agent frameworks and techniques including autoresearch, multi-layer memory systems, and self-evolving skill mechanisms. The creator demonstrates practical implementations with Claude and compares self-evolution approaches across platforms like Hermes and Openclaw.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 in three sizes: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models combine hybrid Mamba-Transformer architecture with Latent Mixture of Experts and Multitask Token Prediction techniques to improve performance and efficiency.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model that replaces full attention with MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA). The model includes KV cache optimizations and tiled I/O improvements that reduce latency during prefill and decode stages. M3 is available via the MiniMax platform with API access and token subscription plans.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is presented as a unique agent framework that enables novel use cases and harness layers for AI applications. The video compares Pi's approach to frontier agent tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex, examining its framework design and practical applications.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Huawei's announcement of the Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the hardware race between China and the USA. The video covers advances in EUV and DUV lithography, chip packaging, and logic folding techniques driving the semiconductor industry forward.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses job hunting strategies in 2026 as AI screening becomes standard. The video argues that networking and personal branding remain valuable, junior developers still bring real value to teams, and that jobs evolve rather than disappear entirely.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: The video outlines five best practices for deploying TypeScript AI agents to production: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication with API Gateway and Cognito, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails and cost controls via lifecycle hooks and model routing, and observability through metrics and OpenTelemetry tracing. These patterns apply across platforms but are demonstrated using AWS services and the Strands Agents SDK.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: AWS CDK is an Infrastructure as Code framework that lets developers define AWS cloud resources using programming languages like Python. The video demonstrates building a serverless REST API using CDK to replace manual AWS console configuration of DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway, enabling single-command deployment and teardown.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrates five techniques to reduce AI hallucinations in agent systems: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise structured queries, multi-agent validation to catch failures, symbolic guardrails via code rather than prompts, and runtime agent steering for self-correction. The methods are applied to a travel agent built with Strands Agents and shown to work with multiple LLM providers.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A YouTube video examining whether Google engineers use AI-assisted code generation tools. The content compares OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities and surveys the adoption of "vibe coding" practices at Google across 2024-2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or tool, arguing it represents a threat to open source software. The video explores how Malus operates and debates whether it is fundamentally "evil" or a legitimate shift in how open source maintainers should be compensated.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: A creator compared Claude Code's performance on a coding task against their own work, finding the AI outperformed them. The video documents the experiment setup, workflow challenges, terminal interactions, and results while raising questions about AI-driven job replacement.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early adopters do not signal real product-market fit, which instead manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He advocates skipping formal interviews in favor of embedded customer relationships and betting on AI model trajectories rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, discusses his decision to build an ad monetization platform for AI tools after raising $26M. He argues that advertising, not subscriptions, will sustain AI applications, and identifies human trust in AI agents as the real bottleneck rather than technology itself.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapped growth playbook from zero to $10M ARR, covering strategies like product-led growth, SEO optimization, churn reduction, and pricing experimentation. The episode includes his decision-making framework and reflections on when to raise capital versus remain independent.

videos.title: A CS Professor on Why Slow Learning Wins in the AI Era | CU Boulder, Tom Yeh
videos.summary: Tom Yeh, Associate Professor at CU Boulder, argues that teaching AI by hand at human speed and focusing on mathematical foundations rather than latest tools creates durable skills that survive hype cycles. He contends that people, not AI, drive meaningful change in AI development.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
